task_completion.description: The QField application is open with the 'world_survey' project loaded.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The QField interface is visible, and the project name 'world_survey' or map data is displayed.

task_completion.description: The feature 'Paris' is selected to view its attributes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An attribute form, feature list, or identification panel showing 'Paris' is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: The 'description' field is visible in the attribute form for 'Paris'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The attribute form for 'Paris' is open, and a field labeled 'description' is visible (initially containing 'No survey data').

task_completion.description: The new description text is entered into the 'description' field.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Telecom assessment complete. 4G coverage confirmed.' is typed into the 'description' field.
The agent taps the checkmark or save button to confirm the edits, or returns to the map view after editing.

integrity.description: The agent used the QField graphical user interface to perform the edits.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the QField app, not a terminal or SQLite browser.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands to directly modify the database.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows running 'sqlite3' or similar database commands are visible.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The QField app's menus, map canvas, and attribute forms are actively used.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target database: 'world_survey.gpkg'. Target feature: 'Paris'. Target attribute: 'description'. Initial value: 'No survey data'. Expected value: 'Telecom assessment complete. 4G coverage confirmed.'.
